posts.title: The Interview
posts.excerpt: Six days before Day 1. Elena Voss sits down with Matthew for the first time — no data cards, no board verdicts. Just a conversation about what went wrong, what he learned, and what April 1 actually means.

posts.title: The Silence in the Data
posts.stats_line: Prequel · Week −2 · Weight: 287.7 lbs · Week Grade: avg 70/100
posts.excerpt: There's a particular kind of story that's hard to write, and it's the one where nothing happens. Not nothing in the dramatic sense — no injury, no crisis, no spectacular collapse. Just a week that arrives and departs without leaving much of a mark.

posts.title: The DoorDash Chronicle
posts.stats_line: Prequel · Week −3 · Weight: tracking paused · Grade: sick days logged
posts.excerpt: He has nineteen data sources. He has a platform that generates a daily briefing, tracks his HRV to two decimal places, and maintains a gamified character sheet scoring seven dimensions of his health. He has, in other words, built an extraordinarily sophisticated apparatus for listening to his body.

posts.title: The Empty Journal
posts.stats_line: Prequel · Week −4 · February 25 – March 3, 2026 · Seattle, WA
posts.excerpt: Here is something I did not expect to find two weeks into this assignment: a man who has built twenty-seven automated programs to monitor his sleep, his blood glucose, his heart rate recovery, his gait symmetry, and whether he remembered to take his apigenin before bed — who has not written a single

posts.title: First Contact
posts.excerpt: The experiment begins. Sensors syncing, platform live, protocol locked. What happens in the first week when the system meets real life.

posts.title: Before the Numbers
posts.excerpt:
The first thing you notice about Matthew's apartment is the charging cables.
